channel.title: Linear Algebra on Eric Windsor
channel.description: Recent content in Linear Algebra on Eric Windsor

item.title: Diagonalization
item.description: Diagonalisation An n × n matrix A is diagonalisable if it is similar to a diagonal matrix: that is if there exists an invertible n × n matrix C and a diagonal matrix D such that $A= CDC^{−1}$. Compute powers of a diagonalisable matrix. If $A= CDC^{−1}$, where $D$ is a diagonal matrix, then $A^n = CD^nC^{−1}$: $$ A = C \begin{bmatrix} x &0 &0\\\\ 0 &y &0\\\\ 0 &0 &z \end{bmatrix} C^{-1} \iff A^n = C \begin{bmatrix} x^n &0 &0\\\\ 0 &y^n &0\\\\ 0 &0 &z^n \end{bmatrix} C^{-1} $$

item.title: Linear Algebra Week1&2
item.description: Data Science: Math + CS + Business AI: Let the computer make an intellectual task as well as a human being (e.g.: Chess game [Decison Tree]) Machine Learning: Teaching skills via data. Data learn how to make the task itself via a specific algorithm (e.g.: Netflix recommendation engine) Neural Network: Each point link each other and each of them is a small computer Deep Learning: Neural Network + several layers in the middle

item.title: Geometry
item.description: Three equivalent ways of thinking about a linear system: As a system of equations: $$ \begin{cases} 2x + 3x_2 − 2x_3 = 7\\\\ x_1− x_2− 3x_3= 5 \end{cases} $$ As an augmented matrix: $$ \left( \begin{array}{ccc} 2 & 3 & -2 \\\\ 1 & -1 & -3 \end{array} \middle| \begin{array}{c} 7 \\\\ 5 \end{array} \right) $$ As a vector equation $(x_1v_1 + x_2v_2 + ··· + x_nv_n = b)$: $$ x_1\begin{pmatrix}2\\\\1\end{pmatrix}+x_2\begin{pmatrix}3\\\\-1\end{pmatrix}+x_3\begin{pmatrix}-2\\\\-3\end{pmatrix}=\begin{pmatrix}7\\\\5\end{pmatrix} $$ Span Given a set of vectors ${v_1, v_2, \ldots, v_n}$ in a vector space $V$, the span of that set, denoted by $\text{Span}{v_1, v_2, \ldots, v_n}$, is the set of all linear combinations of those vectors: $$ \text{Span}\lbrace v_1, v_2, \ldots, v_n\rbrace = \lbrace a_1v_1 + a_2v_2 + \ldots + a_nv_n \mid a_1, a_2, \ldots, a_n \in \mathbb{R}\rbrace $$ In other words, the span is the set of all possible vectors that can be written as a linear combination of the given vectors.

item.title: System of linear equations
item.description: Linear equations An equation in the unknowns $x, y,z,…$ is called linear if both sides of the equation are a sum of (constant) multiples of $x, y,z,…,$ plus an optional constant. For instance, $3x + 4y = 2z$ and $−x − z = 100$. A system of linear equations is a collection of several linear equations, as below: $$ \begin{cases} x + 2y = -3 \\\\ x + 2y = 3 \end{cases} $$ A system of equations is called inconsistent if it has no solutions.
